votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq functions and specification, preserving the logic for factorial, sum, list generation, and the properties checked in the spec.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, including the recursive functions for factorial, sum, list generation, and the specification checking the list properties.
The Python code faithfully translates the Coq definitions and specification, preserving the logic for factorial, sum, list generation, and the validation checks.

The Python code correctly translates the Coq definitions. factorial, sum_1_to_n, f_element, and f_list all compute the same values for concrete inputs. The f_spec function checks the same conditions: result equals f_list(n), length equals n, and each element at position i-1 equals f_element(i). The parity check (i % 2 == 0) matches Nat.even since Coq's Nat.even returns true for even natural numbers. The default value 0 in the Python nth access matches Coq's nth default of 0.

The Python code correctly translates the Coq definitions. factorial, sum_1_to_n, f_element, and f_list all produce the same concrete results. The f_spec function checks the same conditions: result equals f_list(n), length equals n, and each element at index i-1 equals f_element(i) for i from 1 to n. The Python returns a boolean while Coq uses Prop, but they agree on concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly translates the Coq definitions. factorial, sum_1_to_n, f_element, and f_list all compute the same values for natural number inputs. The f_spec function checks the same conditions: result equals f_list(n), length equals n, and each element at position i-1 equals f_element(i) for i from 1 to n. The Coq Nat.even checks if a nat is even, which matches Python's i % 2 == 0 for non-negative integers. The nth function with default 0 matches the Python fallback logic.
Matches Coq for natural-number inputs: factorial and sum recursion, even/odd choice, list construction, and element checks align; f_spec verifies the same properties elementwise.

For all natural-number inputs, the Python functions compute the same factorial, sum, parity-based selection, list construction, and f_spec checks (including default 0 for out-of-bounds nth) as the Coq definitions.
Python mirrors the Coq logic for natural numbers: same factorial and sum recursions, parity check, list construction, and spec verification (including nth with default).
